{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Auditor",
  "description": "Bakangizo Pharmacy and Stores is a proudly Nigerian, multi-service retail brand committed to meeting the everyday needs of our customers through quality products, excellent service, and community-focused operations. We operate a unique combination of businesses under one roof, making us a convenient and trusted destination for families and individuals alike.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Auditor&nbsp;

Job Locations: Nasarawa and Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities


	Conduct routine and surprise audits of branch operations, cash, inventory, and financial records.
	Verify compliance with company policies, procedures, and internal controls.
	Identify operational inefficiencies, fraud risks, and control weaknesses, and recommend corrective actions.
	Audit stock movements, inventory reconciliation, and warehouse processes.
	Review sales, purchases, expenses, and bank reconciliations for accuracy.
	Prepare detailed audit reports and present findings to Management.
	Follow up on audit recommendations to ensure timely implementation.
	Support investigations into financial discrepancies, stock variances, and policy violations.
	Monitor adherence to regulatory and company compliance requirements.
	Perform any other audit assignments as directed by Management.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 2&ndash;3 years of experience in internal audit, preferably in the retail, pharmaceutical, FMCG, or supermarket sector.
	Strong understanding of accounting principles, internal controls, and audit procedures.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software.
	Excellent analytical, investigative, and report-writing skills.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and attention to detail.
	Professional certification (ACA, ACCA, ICAN, ANAN, or CIA) is an added advantage.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hiring436@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Auditor Job at Bakangizo Pharmacy and Stores




        
    

        
        Bakangizo Pharmacy and Stores is a proudly Nigerian, multi-service retail brand committed to meeting the everyday needs of our customers through quality products, excellent service, and community-focused operations. We operate a unique combination of businesses under one roof, making us a convenient and trusted destination for families and individuals alike.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Auditor 

Job Locations: Nasarawa and Rivers
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

            
    




	Conduct routine and surprise audits of branch operations, cash, inventory, and financial records.
	Verify compliance with company policies, procedures, and internal controls.
	Identify operational inefficiencies, fraud risks, and control weaknesses, and recommend corrective actions.
	Audit stock movements, inventory reconciliation, and warehouse processes.
	Review sales, purchases, expenses, and bank reconciliations for accuracy.
	Prepare detailed audit reports and present findings to Management.
	Follow up on audit recommendations to ensure timely implementation.
	Support investigations into financial discrepancies, stock variances, and policy violations.
	Monitor adherence to regulatory and company compliance requirements.
	Perform any other audit assignments as directed by Management.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Accounting, Finance, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 2–3 years of experience in internal audit, preferably in the retail, pharmaceutical, FMCG, or supermarket sector.
	Strong understanding of accounting principles, internal controls, and audit procedures.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and accounting software.
	Excellent analytical, investigative, and report-writing skills.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and attention to detail.
	Professional certification (ACA, ACCA, ICAN, ANAN, or CIA) is an added advantage.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Business Development Executive",
  "description": "Kaizen Microfinance Bank Limited is a customer-centric financial institution committed to providing innovative, accessible, and reliable banking solutions to individuals, small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), and corporate clients. Licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), the Bank is dedicated to promoting financial inclusion by delivering a broad range of products and services tailored to meet the evolving needs of its customers.

Driven by excellence, integrity, innovation, and exceptional service delivery, Kaizen Microfinance Bank leverages technology and a highly skilled workforce to empower businesses, support economic growth, and create lasting value for its stakeholders. At Kaizen MFB, we believe banking should be simple, secure, and focused on helping every customer achieve sustainable financial success.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Business Development Executive
Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	
	Kaizen Microfinance Bank is seeking dynamic, results-driven, and customer-focused Business Development Executives to drive&nbsp;&nbsp;business growth by sourcing new customers, mobilizing deposits, marketing the Bank&#39;s products and services, and building strong customer relationships.
	


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and acquire new customers for the Bank&#39;s products and services.
	Mobilize deposits and achieve assigned business targets.
	Market loan products and facilitate quality loan origination.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with customers.
	Conduct market visits and identify new business opportunities.
	Ensure excellent customer service and prompt resolution of customer issues.
	Prepare and submit periodic sales and activity reports.
	Comply with the Bank&#39;s policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND/HND/Bachelor&#39;s Degree in any discipline.
	Previous experience in sales, marketing, or business development within the financial services industry is an added advantage.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Self-motivated, target-driven, and able to work with minimal supervision.
	Good knowledge of Microsoft Office applications.


Remuneration


	Very Attractive salary.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: chinelo.nmema@kaizenng.com using&quot;Business Development Executive&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Business Development Executive Job at Kaizen Microfinance Bank Limited




        
    

        
        Kaizen Microfinance Bank Limited is a customer-centric financial institution committed to providing innovative, accessible, and reliable banking solutions to individuals, small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs), and corporate clients. Licensed by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), the Bank is dedicated to promoting financial inclusion by delivering a broad range of products and services tailored to meet the evolving needs of its customers.
     

            
    



Driven by excellence, integrity, innovation, and exceptional service delivery, Kaizen Microfinance Bank leverages technology and a highly skilled workforce to empower businesses, support economic growth, and create lasting value for its stakeholders. At Kaizen MFB, we believe banking should be simple, secure, and focused on helping every customer achieve sustainable financial success.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Business Development Executive
Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	
	Kaizen Microfinance Bank is seeking dynamic, results-driven, and customer-focused Business Development Executives to drive  business growth by sourcing new customers, mobilizing deposits, marketing the Bank's products and services, and building strong customer relationships.
	


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and acquire new customers for the Bank's products and services.
	Mobilize deposits and achieve assigned business targets.
	Market loan products and facilitate quality loan origination.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with customers.
	Conduct market visits and identify new business opportunities.
	Ensure excellent customer service and prompt resolution of customer issues.
	Prepare and submit periodic sales and activity reports.
	Comply with the Bank's policies, procedures, and regulatory requirements.


Requirements


	Minimum of OND/HND/Bachelor's Degree in any discipline.
	Previous experience in sales, marketing, or business development within the financial services industry is an added advantage.
	Strong communication, negotiation, and interpersonal skills.
	Self-motivated, target-driven, and able to work with minimal supervision.
	Good knowledge of Microsoft Office applications.


Remuneration


	Very Attractive salary.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using"Business Development Executive" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Export & Warehouse Team Lead",
  "description": "EFL Limited is a bonded, Off-dock terminal and off-dock solution offering a host of terminal and logistics services to Shippers and Carriers alike.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Export &amp; Warehouse Team Lead
Job Location: Ikorodu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role


	We are seeking an experienced and results-driven Export &amp; Warehouse Team Lead to oversee warehouse operations and export stuffing activities.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for ensuring efficient inventory management, seamless export operations, optimal warehouse utilization, and excellent customer service while leading a high-performing operations team.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee daily warehouse and export stuffing operations.
	Manage inventory receiving, storage, movement, and dispatch while maintaining high inventory accuracy.
	Coordinate export stuffing activities and ensure timely loading of export containers.
	Develop, implement, and enforce warehouse processes, policies, and security procedures.
	Review MSDS documentation and ensure appropriate storage conditions for incoming cargo.
	Liaise with customers to ensure efficient truck unloading and container stuffing.
	Monitor the movement of empty and stuffed containers within the terminal.
	Ensure all stuffed containers are properly sealed before dispatch.
	Oversee weighbridge activities and promptly resolve operational discrepancies.
	Prepare and submit daily stuffing, weighbridge, inventory, and incident reports.
	Lead and coordinate warehouse supervisors, equipment operators, labour personnel, and customer representatives.
	Drive continuous improvements in warehouse productivity, safety, and operational efficiency.


Requirements


	HND or Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 7 years of experience in warehouse management, export operations, logistics, or container terminal operations.
	Professional certifications in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Export Operations, or Project Management are an added advantage.
	Proven experience leading warehouse teams and managing export operations.
	Sound knowledge of logistics and supply chain operations, warehouse management, inventory control, and container terminal operations.
	Experience using warehouse management systems (WMS), Microsoft Excel, and preparing operational reports.
	Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.
	Strong customer service orientation with the ability to build effective working relationships.
	High level of attention to detail and commitment to inventory accuracy.
	Ability to&nbsp;work under pressure in a fast-paced operational environment.
	Proactive, adaptable, and highly organized with a strong focus on safety, quality, and continuous improvement.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-07-20",
  "validThrough": "2026-08-16",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Export & Warehouse Team Lead Job at EFL Limited




        
    

        
        EFL Limited is a bonded, Off-dock terminal and off-dock solution offering a host of terminal and logistics services to Shippers and Carriers alike.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Export & Warehouse Team Lead
Job Location: Ikorodu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

About the Role
     

            
    




	We are seeking an experienced and results-driven Export & Warehouse Team Lead to oversee warehouse operations and export stuffing activities.
	The successful candidate will be responsible for ensuring efficient inventory management, seamless export operations, optimal warehouse utilization, and excellent customer service while leading a high-performing operations team.


Key Responsibilities


	Oversee daily warehouse and export stuffing operations.
	Manage inventory receiving, storage, movement, and dispatch while maintaining high inventory accuracy.
	Coordinate export stuffing activities and ensure timely loading of export containers.
	Develop, implement, and enforce warehouse processes, policies, and security procedures.
	Review MSDS documentation and ensure appropriate storage conditions for incoming cargo.
	Liaise with customers to ensure efficient truck unloading and container stuffing.
	Monitor the movement of empty and stuffed containers within the terminal.
	Ensure all stuffed containers are properly sealed before dispatch.
	Oversee weighbridge activities and promptly resolve operational discrepancies.
	Prepare and submit daily stuffing, weighbridge, inventory, and incident reports.
	Lead and coordinate warehouse supervisors, equipment operators, labour personnel, and customer representatives.
	Drive continuous improvements in warehouse productivity, safety, and operational efficiency.


Requirements


	HND or Bachelor's Degree in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, or a related discipline.
	Minimum of 7 years of experience in warehouse management, export operations, logistics, or container terminal operations.
	Professional certifications in Logistics, Supply Chain Management, Export Operations, or Project Management are an added advantage.
	Proven experience leading warehouse teams and managing export operations.
	Sound knowledge of logistics and supply chain operations, warehouse management, inventory control, and container terminal operations.
	Experience using warehouse management systems (WMS), Microsoft Excel, and preparing operational reports.
	Strong leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills.
	Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.
	Strong customer service orientation with the ability to build effective working relationships.
	High level of attention to detail and commitment to inventory accuracy.
	Ability to work under pressure in a fast-paced operational environment.
	Proactive, adaptable, and highly organized with a strong focus on safety, quality, and continuous improvement.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance Administrator",
  "description": "EFL Limited is a bonded, Off-dock terminal and off-dock solution offering a host of terminal and logistics services to Shippers and Carriers alike.

We are&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Finance Administrator

Job Location: Ikorodu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;

About the Role


	We are looking for a detail-oriented and analytical Finance Administrator to support the Finance team in maintaining accurate financial records, processing transactions, and ensuring compliance with company financial procedures.
	The ideal candidate will have strong accounting knowledge, excellent numerical skills, and experience using accounting software.


Key Responsibilities


	Journalize financial transactions using accounting software (Xero).
	Conduct monthly bank reconciliations, account reconciliations, and financial record reviews to ensure accuracy and resolve discrepancies.
	Compute and analyze barge operational data for financial reporting purposes.
	Prepare and issue detailed customer receipts for financial transactions.
	Review payment vouchers against bank statements to ensure accuracy and completeness.
	Assist in preparing and monitoring monthly direct costs.
	Review and verify invoices before processing.
	Maintain accurate financial records and supporting documentation.
	Support the Finance Manager with routine financial reporting and administrative tasks.
	Perform other finance-related duties as assigned by the Finance Manager.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree or HND in Accounting, Finance or a related discipline.
	2&ndash;4 years of experience in accounting, finance, or a similar role.
	Experience using Xero Accounting Software is highly preferred.
	Strong knowledge of bookkeeping, bank reconciliation, financial reporting, and accounting principles.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other Microsoft Office applications.
	Relevant professional qualifications (ICAN, ACCA, ATS, or equivalent) are an added advantage.
	Strong analytical and numerical skills with excellent attention to detail.
	Ability to reconcile financial records accurately and identify discrepancies.
	Good understanding of financial controls and accounting procedures.
	Excellent organizational and time management skills.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and professionalism.
	To&nbsp;work independently and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

Finance Administrator Job at EFL Limited




        
    

        
        EFL Limited is a bonded, Off-dock terminal and off-dock solution offering a host of terminal and logistics services to Shippers and Carriers alike.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Finance Administrator

Job Location: Ikorodu, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time 

About the Role
     

            
    




	We are looking for a detail-oriented and analytical Finance Administrator to support the Finance team in maintaining accurate financial records, processing transactions, and ensuring compliance with company financial procedures.
	The ideal candidate will have strong accounting knowledge, excellent numerical skills, and experience using accounting software.


Key Responsibilities


	Journalize financial transactions using accounting software (Xero).
	Conduct monthly bank reconciliations, account reconciliations, and financial record reviews to ensure accuracy and resolve discrepancies.
	Compute and analyze barge operational data for financial reporting purposes.
	Prepare and issue detailed customer receipts for financial transactions.
	Review payment vouchers against bank statements to ensure accuracy and completeness.
	Assist in preparing and monitoring monthly direct costs.
	Review and verify invoices before processing.
	Maintain accurate financial records and supporting documentation.
	Support the Finance Manager with routine financial reporting and administrative tasks.
	Perform other finance-related duties as assigned by the Finance Manager.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree or HND in Accounting, Finance or a related discipline.
	2–4 years of experience in accounting, finance, or a similar role.
	Experience using Xero Accounting Software is highly preferred.
	Strong knowledge of bookkeeping, bank reconciliation, financial reporting, and accounting principles.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other Microsoft Office applications.
	Relevant professional qualifications (ICAN, ACCA, ATS, or equivalent) are an added advantage.
	Strong analytical and numerical skills with excellent attention to detail.
	Ability to reconcile financial records accurately and identify discrepancies.
	Good understanding of financial controls and accounting procedures.
	Excellent organizational and time management skills.
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and professionalism.
	To work independently and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
